fault_hypothesis: On line 74, the mean is computed by dividing `total` by `window` instead of `len(recent)` (i.e., `effective_window`). When the window is larger than the number of available values, this produces an incorrect (too small) result.

constraints.intent: After computing mean, it should equal total divided by the number of recent samples, not by window.
mean times the actual sample count must equal total; fails when window > len(values).
Confirms that effective_window clamps len(recent) to at most window, so dividing by window instead of len(recent) causes error when they differ.

spec.expr: __cbfl_result is None or abs(__cbfl_result - sum(values[-min(window, len(values)):]) / min(window, len(values))) < 1e-9

constraints.intent: Return value must equal the mean of the actual recent samples, not total divided by the full window size.
